home.title: VEINES-QOL/Sym
home.intro: A patient-owned tracker for the Venous Insufficiency Epidemiological and Economic Study quality-of-life questionnaire. Take the survey at baseline and at follow-ups to see how your symptoms and quality of life change over time.
home.resume.title: Resume in progress
home.resume.body: You have a draft survey from {date}. Your answers are saved on this device.
home.start.title: Start a survey
home.start.body: About 25 questions. Takes 5–7 minutes. You can pause and resume anytime — your answers are saved as you go, on this device only.
home.prior.title: Your prior result
survey.incomplete: Please answer the remaining questions (need {threshold}% items).
results.empty.title: No results yet
results.empty.body: Once you complete a survey, your scores will appear here.
results.title: Your VEINES result
results.subtitle: Completed {date}. Higher = better.
results.qol.label: Quality of life (T-score)
results.sym.label: Symptoms (T-score)
results.share: Share this with your provider — they understand what these numbers mean for your care.
results.export.hint: Opens your device's print/save dialog. Practice-branded, one page.
chart.aria.summary: Trend chart of {count} score(s). Latest: QOL {qol}, Symptoms {sym}.
install.title: Save to Home Screen
install.body: iOS Safari may delete your saved scores after 7 days unless you install this app to your Home Screen.
install.step1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of Safari
install.step2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
install.step3: Tap Add. Reopen this app from your Home Screen.
reminder.title: Follow-up reminder
reminder.body: Your follow-up was due {days} day(s) ago. Taking it now keeps your trend complete.
notification.permission.body: We'll notify you when each follow-up is due. No tracking — the schedule lives on this device.
swupdate.title: A new version is available
swupdate.body.busy: Finish your survey first, then refresh.
swupdate.body.ready: Reload to get the latest improvements.
print.report.title: VEINES-QOL/Sym score report
print.report.subtitle: Patient-administered. Higher T-score = better.
print.report.citation: Instrument: Lamping DL et al. J Vasc Surg 2003;37:410-419. Scoring: Kahn SR et al. J Clin Epidemiol 2006;59:1049-1056.
print.report.dataownership: Data is patient-controlled and stored only on the patient's device. This report was exported by the patient and shared by them with this clinician.
item.Q1.1: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have heavy legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.2: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have aching legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.3: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have leg swelling in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.4: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have leg cramps at night in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.5: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have a heat or burning sensation in your legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.6: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have restless legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.7: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have throbbing in your legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.8: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have itching legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q1.9: [PLACEHOLDER] How often did you have tingling in your legs in the past 4 weeks?
item.Q3: [PLACEHOLDER] Over the past year, your leg problems have:
item.Q4.1: [PLACEHOLDER] Daily activities at work — limited by leg problems?
item.Q4.2: [PLACEHOLDER] Daily activities at home — limited by leg problems?
item.Q4.3: [PLACEHOLDER] Social or leisure activities — limited by leg problems?
item.Q5: [PLACEHOLDER] In the past 4 weeks, how often did your leg problems make you feel depressed?
item.Q6: [PLACEHOLDER] In the past 4 weeks, how often did you feel worried about the appearance of your legs?
item.Q7: [PLACEHOLDER] In the past 4 weeks, how often did your leg problems make you feel irritable?
item.Q8: [PLACEHOLDER] In the past 4 weeks, how often did your leg problems interfere with your social life?
